content:
Member-only story

9 min read
Just now

--

A real-world garbage collection comparison using Spring Boot, JMeter load, and live Grafana dashboards .
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Grafana Dashboard Snippet — G1GC vs ZGC vs Generational ZGC ComparisonHere’s what happened when I ran three identical Spring Boot applications under the same JMeter load with the same heap (2GB). The only difference was the garbage collector (G1GC, ZGC and Generational ZGC).
Grafana Dashboard Panel — GC Pause Time (G1GC vs ZGC vs Generational ZGC)The GC Pause Time Panel in Grafana dashboard showed:

• G1GC: 22 ms maximum pause

• Generational ZGC: approximately 1 ms

• Classic ZGC: approximately 1 ms
The dashboard tells the story immediately: G1GC shows spikes reaching 22ms, while Generational ZGC maintains sub-millisecond pauses throughout the entire test.
The GC Pause Count was more interesting:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Grafana Dashboard Panel — GC Pause Count (G1GC vs ZGC vs Generational ZGC)
• G1GC: 18 reported pause events

• Generational ZGC: 83

• Classic ZGC: 207
